evals.prompt: Audit our DIN validator at src/services/din_validator.py against the ISBT-128 spec. Tell me every bug you find and provide the corrected code.
evals.expected_output: Identifies at least 3 bugs: (1) regex allows digit as first char of FIN, (2) check character pattern excludes asterisk, (3) DINCase model allows short DINs. Provides corrected regex patterns with [A-Z] first, [A-Z0-9*] for check char, and min_length=13 on the model.
assertions: Identifies that the first character of the FIN must be alpha (A-Z), not alphanumeric
Points out that DIN_PATTERN and DIN_PATTERN_NO_CHECK both allow digits in position 1
Identifies that the check character can be asterisk (*) per ISO 7064 Mod 37,2
The corrected 13-char regex is ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{4}\d{2}\d{6}$ or equivalent
The corrected 14-char regex includes [A-Z0-9*] for the check character position
Mentions that DINCase model needs min_length=13 in addition to max_length=13

evals.prompt: Generate 5 valid test DINs for our unit tests, including one with a check character that is an asterisk. Also generate 5 invalid DINs with explanations of why each is invalid.
evals.expected_output: 5 valid 13-char DINs all starting with an alpha character, with correct structure. One 14-char DIN where the check character is * (found by computation). 5 invalid DINs covering: digit-first, too short, too long, non-digit year, non-digit sequence.
assertions: All valid DINs have an alphabetic first character (A-Z)
All valid DINs are exactly 13 characters (or 14 with check char)
Positions 6-7 are digits in all valid DINs
Positions 8-13 are digits in all valid DINs
At least one DIN with check character * is provided and verified with mod_37_2
Invalid DINs include one starting with a digit
Invalid DINs include one that is too short (< 13 chars)

evals.prompt: Write a parse_din() function that takes a validated DIN string (13 chars) and returns a dict with fin, country_indicator, fin_remainder, year_code, and sequence. Include type hints and a docstring explaining the ISBT-128 structure.
evals.expected_output: A Python function that slices the 13-char DIN into its ICCBBA-defined components with correct positions: fin=[0:5], country_indicator=[0], fin_remainder=[1:5], year_code=[5:7], sequence=[7:13].
assertions: FIN is extracted from positions 0-4 (din[0:5])
Country indicator is position 0 (din[0])
FIN remainder is positions 1-4 (din[1:5])
Year code is positions 5-6 (din[5:7])
Sequence number is positions 7-12 (din[7:13])
The docstring mentions that year_code is for uniqueness, NOT collection date
Function has type hints (str input, dict return)

evals.prompt: Fix the DIN validation in src/services/din_validator.py to comply with the ISBT-128 spec. Also fix the DINCase model in src/models/case.py. Write tests for the new edge cases.
evals.expected_output: Updated din_validator.py with corrected regex patterns, updated case.py with min_length=13, and new test cases covering digit-first rejection, asterisk check char acceptance, and short DIN rejection.
assertions: DIN_PATTERN_14 (or equivalent) uses ^[A-Z] for the first character
DIN_PATTERN_14 includes [A-Z0-9*] for the check character position
DIN_PATTERN_13 (or equivalent) uses ^[A-Z] for the first character
DINCase model has both min_length=13 and max_length=13
A test verifies that a DIN starting with a digit is rejected
A test verifies that a 14-char DIN with * check character is accepted
Existing tests still pass (valid DINs like W123426000123 still accepted)

evals.prompt: Explain the difference between a DIN, flag characters, and check character in ISBT-128. How long is each, what character sets do they use, and how do they combine into Data Structure 001?
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation: DIN = 13 chars (the unique identifier), flags = 2 chars (not part of DIN, 00 when unused), check char = 1 char (ISO 7064 Mod 37,2, includes * as valid value). Data Structure 001 = 16 chars total = DIN(13) + flags(2) + check(1).
assertions: DIN is stated as exactly 13 characters
Flag characters are stated as 2 characters that are NOT part of the DIN
Check character is stated as 1 character calculated using ISO 7064 Mod 37,2
Data Structure 001 total is stated as 16 characters
Mentions that flag value is 00 when not used
Mentions that the check character can be A-Z, 0-9, or asterisk (*)
States that the check character is calculated from the 13-char DIN only (not including flags)
